plan.name: Brightspeed FY2026 Unified Compensation Plan
plan.description: This plan spans 5 distinct channels with different processing frequencies, crediting bases, and rate structures. Recommend implementing as 5 separate CaptivateIQ plans sharing common data sources and attribute worksheets. D2D and Indirect share similar structure (speed-tiered rates, clawback logic) but differ in crediting basis (issued vs installed) and rate levels. Digital Partners use a fundamentally different rate structure (matrix by Sales ID). MDU is an internal employee plan with quota-based attainment.

formulaReference._note: CaptivateIQ SmartGrid formulas are defined in the UI and are NOT accessible via the REST API. Use the pseudo-formulas and CaptivateIQ notes below as a reference when manually building the calculation logic in the plan builder.

formulas.logic_explanation: For each order, determine if it has a download speed. If yes, look up the speed-tiered rate from the appropriate rate table filtered by partner group (D2D) or order type (Indirect). If no speed, look up the flat product rate from the VAS/Voice table. D2D uses partner group dimension; Indirect uses order type dimension.
formulas.pseudo_formula: IF(DownloadSpeed IS BLANK, LOOKUP(Product + '|' + PartnerGroupOrOrderType, VAS_Voice_Rate_Table.Rate), LOOKUP_APPROX(DownloadSpeed, IF(Channel='D2D', D2D_Speed_Rate_Table[PartnerGroup_Column], Indirect_Speed_Rate_Table[OrderType_Column])))
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Use SmartGrid VLOOKUP with approximate match for speed-tiered rates. Create two lookup columns in the Payout Worksheet — one for speed-based and one for product-based — then use IF to select. Filter the rate table by PartnerGroup or OrderType before lookup.

formulas.logic_explanation: For Digital/DAP channel, lookup is by SalesID. First determine which rate column applies using the Product Name Mapping (OrderType + Technology + ProductName → RateColumn). Then look up the rate from the Digital Partner Rate Matrix at the intersection of SalesID and RateColumn.
formulas.pseudo_formula: LET(rateColumn = LOOKUP(OrderType + Technology + ProductName, DigitalProductMapping.RateColumn), LOOKUP(SalesID, DigitalPartnerRateMatrix[rateColumn]))
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Implement as a two-step lookup in SmartGrid. Step 1: Map the product to a rate column using the Product Name Mapping worksheet. Step 2: Retrieve the rate from the Digital Partner Rate Matrix for the given SalesID and rate column. Consider using the normalized (unpivoted) rate table format for simpler VLOOKUP.

formulas.logic_explanation: Apply a series of eligibility gates: (1) Technology != 'Copper' for D2D, (2) OrderDate >= 2025-09-06 for all channels, (3) Channel matches plan assignment, (4) Partner is Active, (5) Compliance flag is not 'OWS - Do Not Pay'. Return Eligible=TRUE only if all conditions pass; otherwise set appropriate FailCode.
formulas.pseudo_formula: Eligible = AND(IF(Channel='D2D', Technology != 'Copper', TRUE), OrderDate >= '2025-09-06', PartnerStatus = 'Active', ComplianceFlag != 'OWS - Do Not Pay')
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Implement as a calculated boolean column in the Payout Worksheet. Chain multiple IF conditions. Use a FailCode integer column alongside for debugging/audit. Apply as a multiplier (0 or 1) to the commission amount.

formulas.logic_explanation: For canceled orders: look up the rate that would have been earned, negate it, then check if the ConsolidatedReason is in the waiver list. If waived, commission = $0. For disconnects: always apply full negative chargeback (no waiver). D2D has a 90-day lookback window. Classify as 'Prior Period' or 'Same Period' based on OrderDate vs WeekStart.
formulas.pseudo_formula: CommissionAmountPre = -1 * RateLookup(product, speed, orderType); CBWaived = IF(EventType='Cancel', ISIN(ConsolidatedReason, CancelWaiverReasons), FALSE); FinalAmount = IF(CBWaived, 0, CommissionAmountPre); PeriodType = IF(OrderDate < WeekStart, 'Prior Period', 'Same Period')
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Create separate commission event rows for chargebacks (negative amounts). Use a CommissionEvent column to distinguish 'Chargeback Cancel' from 'Chargeback Disconnect'. Apply waiver logic via MATCH against the Cancel Waiver Reasons data worksheet. For D2D 90-day lookback, filter chargebacks where CancelDate - OrderDate <= 90.

formulas.logic_explanation: For each AE/Natl AE deal: map Offer Type to Agreement Category, look up the per-unit rate for the role+agreement, multiply by contracted units. Sum across all deals in the month. Apply 300% TSI cap via MIN function. Add $1,000 bonus if 5+ Exclusive/Bulk contracts with 50+ units each.
formulas.pseudo_formula: DealCommission = ContractedUnits * LOOKUP(Role + AgreementCategory, MDU_Rate_Table.EffectiveRate); MonthlyTotal = SUM(DealCommissions); BonusContracts = COUNTIFS(AgreementCategory IN ('Exclusive','Bulk'), ContractedUnits >= 50); Bonus = IF(BonusContracts >= 5, 1000, 0); FinalPayout = MIN(MonthlyTotal + Bonus, 3 * MonthlyTSI)
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Use a SmartGrid formula to map each deal through the agreement type mapping, then look up the rate. Aggregate at the rep-month level. Apply the cap as MIN(calculated, 3 * TSI). The 5-contract bonus requires a COUNTIFS across the rep's monthly deals — implement as a separate calculation row or component.

formulas.logic_explanation: Two components each at 50% TSI: (1) Units Sold: attainment% = units_sold / target, commission = TSI_Sales * attainment%. (2) Penetration: pen_ratio = current_month_pen / prior_month_pen, commission = TSI_Pen * pen_ratio. Total = component1 + component2. Apply 200% TSI cap.
formulas.pseudo_formula: SalesComm = TSI_Sales * (UnitsSold / MonthlyTarget); PenComm = TSI_Pen * (CurrentMonthPen / PriorMonthPen); TotalComm = MIN(SalesComm + PenComm, 2 * MonthlyTSI)
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Create two component columns in the Payout Worksheet. TSI_Sales and TSI_Pen are each 50% of Monthly TSI from Employee Assumptions. Prior Month Penetration should be an Employee Assumption column updated monthly. Apply cap at the total level after summing components.

formulas.description: Outbound/OTM Commission with Advance and Clawback
formulas.logic_explanation: Commission is advanced at order issuance based on the speed-tiered rate table. If the order is subsequently canceled, the full advance is clawed back as a negative amount in the following month's payout. Monthly payout = New Install commissions + Migration commissions - Cancel clawbacks + Dispute adjustments.
formulas.pseudo_formula: AdvancePayment = LOOKUP_APPROX(DownloadSpeed, OutboundSpeedRateTable[OrderType_Column]); NetPaymentDue = IF(OrderStatus = 'Canceled', -1 * AdvancePayment, AdvancePayment); MonthlyPayout = SUM(NetPaymentDue) BY Vendor
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Each order generates either a positive commission (new install/migration) or a negative clawback (cancel). Aggregate by vendor/month for the payout worksheet. Track the OriginalPaymentDate to link clawbacks to their original advance period.

formulas.logic_explanation: After commission is calculated, check the Compliance table for orders flagged 'OWS - Do Not Pay'. If flagged, set CommissionWaived = TRUE and NetCommissionAmount = 0. Track the waived amount separately for reporting.
formulas.pseudo_formula: ComplianceFlag = LOOKUP(OrderID, ComplianceTable.Flag); NetCommission = IF(ComplianceFlag = 'OWS - Do Not Pay', 0, CommissionAmount); WaivedAmount = CommissionAmount - NetCommission
formulas.captivateiq_notes: Implement as a final adjustment column in the Payout Worksheet. Join the Compliance data source by OrderID. The waived amount should appear as a separate line item on the partner statement for transparency.
